content.full_text:
September 28, 1988


Susan Daily
Maricopa City Clerk
P.O. Box 548
Maricopa, CA 93252

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-88-341


Dear Ms. Daily:

You have requested advice about the filing obligations of a
person under the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act.1/ Because you do not indicate that you have
been authorized by the person to request advice, this letter is
considered to be a request for informal assistance.2/

QUESTION

Is an appointee to a city council position who resigns from
office less than 30 days after assuming office required to file an
assuming office statement and a leaving office statement of
economic interests?

CONCLUSION

An appointee to a city council position who assumes office is
required to file an assuming office statement and a leaving office
statement even if the person resigns from office within 30 days of
assuming office.


_____________________

1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/Your letter states only a general question. Therefore, we
consider it to be a request for informal assistance pursuant to
Regulation 18329(c) (copy enclosed). Informal assistance does not
provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 84113; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

• Susan Daily
Page 2

ANALYSIS

Members of city councils are required to file statements of
economic interests. (Section 87200.) Everyone who is appointed
to a city council position is required to file a statement within
10 days after assuming office disclosing his or her investments
and real property interests. (Section 87202.) The obligation to
file an assuming office statement exists regardless of the length
of service. Even though the city council appointee was in office
less than 30 days, he or she still is required to file an assuming
office statement.

Within 30 days after leaving office, each city council member
is required to file a statement disclosing his or her investments,
interests in real property and sources of income. (Section
87204.) A leaving office statement is required regardless of the
length of service.

If you have further questions please call me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: Bruce W. Robeck
Political Reform Consultant

DMG:BWR:kmt

CITY OF

MARICOPA

"GATEWAY TO THE SEA"

Area Code (805)-769-8279                                                Box 548, Maricopa, CA 93252


August 26, 1988


Fair Political Practices
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA    95804-0807

Re: SEI Enclosed

Enclosed please find a copy of Mr. Ron Bracken's resignation from the
City Council of the City of Maricopa. On July 22, 1988 I informed you
that Mr. Bracken had been appointed to fill Ms. Dehn's position on the
council and that he received his SEI to complete on that date. Mr. Bracken
was at that time informed he had 10 days in which to file his statement.
On August 2, 1988 I spoke with Mrs. Bracken and informed her that her
husband's statement was late. She informed me that her husband had talked
to the FPPC and they told him he had thirty days, and she was taking the
SEI to their accountant that day. Mr. Bracken's resignation was accepted
by our City Council on August 14th and became effective that date. Mr.
Bracken was in office less than thirty days and the only official action
he was involved in was the final adoption of an ordinance regarding the
regulation of bicycles and skateboards. I would appreciate your official
ruling as to whether Mr. Bracken must file an assuming and leaving office
SEI.

Mr. Robert Hill was appointed to fill Mr. Brackens position on August
18, 1988. Mr. Hill's SEI is enclosed.

I would appreciate your reply as soon as possible.

Sincerely,

Susan Daily
City Clerk

St. Rt. 2, Box 515
Maricopa, CA 93252
August 8, 1988


Maricopa City Council
City of Maricopa
Maricopa, CA 93252

Dear Council Members:

     When I was a councilman twenty years ago,
it was not a requirement to fill out an Economic
Disclosure Statement. Since I have recently been
appointed to the Council, I find that this Dis-
closure must be filed in 30 days.

     With reluctance, I wish to submit my resignation.
I do not wish to make my private business affairs
common knowledge.

     I do appreciate the confidence that was shown
by this appointment. If I had known previous to
the appointment, I would not have accepted the
position of City Councilman.

                                        Yours truly,


                                        Ronald R. Bracken

CITY OF

MARICOPA

"GATEWAY TO THE SEA"

Area Code (805)-769-8279                                                                                               Box 548, Maricopa, CA 93552


August 26, 1988


Fair Political Practices
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804-0807

Re: SEI Enclosed

Enclosed please find a copy of Mr. Ron Bracken's resignation from the
City Council of the City of Maricopa. On July 22, 1988 I informed you
that Mr. Bracken had been appointed to fill Ms. Dehm's position on the
council and that he received his SEI to complete on that date. Mr. Bracken
was at that time informed he had 10 days in which to file his statement.
On August 2, 1988 I spoke with Mrs. Bracken and informed her that her
husband's statement was late. She informed me that her husband had talked
to the FPPC and they told him he had thirty days, and she was taking the
SEI to their accountant that day.  Mr. Bracken's resignation was accepted
by our City Council on August 18th and became effective that date. Mr.
Bracken was in office less than thirty days and the only official action
he was involved in was the final adoption of an ordinance regarding the
regulation of bicycles and skateboards. I would appreciate your official
ruling as to whether Mr. Bracken must file an assuming and leaving office
SEI.

Mr. Robert Hill was appointed to fill Mr. Brackens position on August
18, 1988. Mr. Hill's SEI is enclosed.

I would appreciate your reply as soon as possible.

Sincerely,

Susan Daily
City Clerk

St. Rt. 2, Box 515
Maricopa, CA 93252
August 8, 1988


Maricopa City Council
City of Maricopa
Maricopa, CA 93252

Dear Council Members:

     When I was a councilman twenty years ago,
it was not a requirement to fill out an Economic
Disclosure Statement. Since I have recently been
appointed to the Council, I find that this Dis-
closure must be filed in 30 days.

     With reluctance, I wish to submit my resignation.
I do not wish to make my private business affairs
common knowledge.

     I do appreciate the confidence that was shown
by this appointment. If I had known previous to
the appointment, I would not have accepted the
position of City Councilman.

                                   Yours truly,

                                   [signature]
                                   Ronald R. Bracken

Is an appointee to a city council position who resigns from 
office less than 30 days after assuming office required to file an 
assuming office statement and a leaving office statement of 
economic interests?

An appointee to a city council position who assumes office is 
required to file an assuming office statement and a leaving office 
statement even if the person resigns from office within 30 days of 
assuming office.
